_readme: Resume state for the newsletter harvest (tools/upkeep-routine.md step 2).
Read this BEFORE a harvest pass; update `last_processed`/`count`/`updated` and COMMIT it after.
This file lives in the repo (not session memory) precisely so a CLOUD agent can clone, resume, and open a PR.
`react-brain lint` cross-checks each `count` against encyclopedia.yaml sources_digested — they must match.
Narrative harvest history (what each pass changed, process learnings) stays in the maintainer's memory ledger.

react-digest.name: React Digest
react-digest.notes: issues slug-titled by featured article; article/depth-driven — durable yield is usually one decision-guide reading. Archive moved: /digests 404s, use /newsletters. Slug numbers are NOT sequential (2316 → 2320 with nothing between)

react-native-rewind.name: React Native Rewind
react-native-rewind.notes: slug-titled; each issue page has an authoritative 'Issue #NN' label (near the title) — use THAT, not sequential guesswork, to resume (confirmed 2026-07-16: RSS + newsletter-rss.xml list newest-first; #48 = 'Zoomable Calendar Grids...', dated 2026-07-06, was the newest available and already fully corroborated in-corpus — RB-E-CALENDARS/RB-E-COMPONENT-LIBS/RB-E-ONDEVICE-AI/RB-E-BUILD/RB-E-DESKTOP/RB-E-ANIMATION between them cover every substantive item across issues #46-48)

native-weekly.name: Native Weekly
native-weekly.notes: nativeweekly.com is a shell — use the beehiiv archive; author also authors rnsec (watch self-promotion); strongest beat = agentic dev tooling

react-weekly.name: React Weekly
react-weekly.notes: site + api.react-weekly.dev are JS-shell/auth-gated — harvest via rss.xml content:encoded (FULL bodies). RSS window = latest 20: a backlog >20 issues loses them permanently (issues 1-7 already unreachable). TanStack-heavy.
